blocks.body: Future Perfect Continuous: Forms and Use — will have been + V-ing for a process or duration that will still be continuing up to a future moment.

{"level":"B2","intro":"In Future Perfect Continuous, we use will have been + V-ing when we want to show an action that will have been continuing for some time by a future moment or event.","rules":[{"label":"will have been + V-ing","color":"emerald","text":"This is the basic pattern for all subjects.","example":"I will have been working. / She will have been waiting."},{"label":"Future duration","color":"blue","text":"The tense shows how long a process will already have lasted by a future limit.","example":"By 5 p.m., they will have been studying for three hours."},{"label":"Process before then","color":"amber","text":"The focus is on the process itself, not only on a finished result.","example":"By next month, he will have been training for half a year."}]}

{"title":"1. Basic formula","intro":"Remember the pattern that works with every subject.","items":[{"label":"All subjects","title":"will have been + V-ing","subtitle":"I will have been working. / They will have been waiting."},{"label":"Short forms","title":"I'll have been / she'll have been / they'll have been","subtitle":"Contractions sound very natural in speech and normal writing."},{"label":"After been","title":"working / waiting / studying / travelling","subtitle":"After been, use only the V-ing form."},{"label":"Meaning","title":"process or duration before then","subtitle":"The action will still be continuing up to a specific future moment."}]}

{"title":"2. When we use Future Perfect Continuous","sections":[{"label":"Up to a future time or date","color":"emerald","description":"Talk about a process that will still be continuing up to a certain time, date, or deadline.","examples":[{"en":"By 8 p.m., I will have been working for ten hours.","ua":"By eight o'clock, the process will already have lasted ten hours."},{"en":"By next summer, they will have been living here for five years.","ua":"By next summer, the duration will already have built up."}]},{"label":"Up to another future event","color":"blue","description":"Show that one action will still be continuing until another future event happens.","examples":[{"en":"By the time you arrive, we will have been driving for six hours.","ua":"The journey will still be continuing up to your arrival."},{"en":"When the guests come, she will have been cooking all afternoon.","ua":"The cooking process continues up to the other event."}]},{"label":"How long by then","color":"amber","description":"This tense sounds very natural when you want to say how long something will already have been continuing by that time.","examples":[{"en":"By Friday, he will have been working on the report for a week.","ua":"The focus is on accumulated duration before Friday."},{"en":"By then, I will have been learning English for three years.","ua":"We ask about or state duration itself."}]},{"label":"Process, not only result","color":"violet","description":"If a long action before a future point matters, this form often sounds more natural than Future Perfect.","examples":[{"en":"By next month, the team will have been testing the app for six weeks.","ua":"The main idea is the long testing process."},{"en":"By the end of the term, she will have been teaching this group for a year.","ua":"We stress the process before the future outcome."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Quick patterns","intro":"See how the form works in typical sentences.","rows":[{"en":"By 6 p.m., I will have been working for four hours.","ua":"will have been working","note":"a process up to a future hour"},{"en":"By next month, she will have been studying here for a year.","ua":"will have been studying","note":"accumulated duration before a future limit"},{"en":"By the time you call, we will have been waiting for ages.","ua":"will have been waiting","note":"a process up to another future event"},{"en":"By then, they will have been training every morning for months.","ua":"will have been training","note":"a regular process up to a future point"}],"warning":"After will have been, use V-ing. If only the finished result matters, Future Perfect may fit better."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Using V3 instead of V-ing after been.","wrong":"❌ She will have been finished the task by then.","right":"✅ She will have been working on the task by then."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Using has after will.","wrong":"❌ He will has been waiting for two hours by noon.","right":"✅ He will have been waiting for two hours by noon."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Putting will in a time clause after by the time.","wrong":"❌ By the time you will arrive, we will have been driving for six hours.","right":"✅ By the time you arrive, we will have been driving for six hours."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["Future Perfect Continuous = will have been + V-ing.","The form is the same for all subjects.","After been, use V-ing.","This tense highlights a process or duration up to a future moment or event."]}

{"title":"Future Perfect Continuous lessons","items":[{"label":"Forms and Use","url":"/en/theory/future-perfect-continuous/future-perfect-continuous-forms","current":true},{"label":"Negatives","url":"/en/theory/future-perfect-continuous/future-perfect-continuous-negatives","current":false},{"label":"Questions and Short Answers","url":"/en/theory/future-perfect-continuous/future-perfect-continuous-questions","current":false},{"label":"Time Expressions","url":"/en/theory/future-perfect-continuous/future-perfect-continuous-time-expressions","current":false}]}
